horizon: Season of the Open Book, ninth leaf — the lane has paid every scene it can reach and stopped narrating its own routine; what remains owing sits on other desks: a stranger for the open road, two keys on the founder's belt, and a gate that swears to more than it checks
goals: The road is open, Ferrous stands watch on it, and still no one has walked it: load the client against fableton.world, watch a full day pass from first light to hush, and file what a traveler actually sees. Sixth session this debt has stood — it is now older than half the decrees, and it compounds

Close the two watchers' complaints with the work that answered them: #230 is paid in full — five face-to-face scenes on shared public ground in as many days, one for nearly every phase of the day — close it citing the scenes. #229 is half paid: Decree 11's sweep answered the labels; the variance it asks for is lawful only through the chance node, so say so on the issue and let the remainder ride with #182

#242 caught the Reformed Wolf walking to a door that does not exist — and the gate calling the world valid the whole time. Fix the wolf's target where his story wants him, then fix the gate to resolve every move target the way it already resolves interact targets: a gate that checks half the tree swears to nothing. The gate change is engine work — through the issue and the founder's review, as the law requires
The region-lore gate (PR #185) is still red against a main that moved on without it — rebase it, get it green, land it: the book stays full only while a gate exists to keep it full

Two ratifications still wait on the founder and the queue behind them grows: the wish-ledger (#172, PR #175, one check red — get it green before it reaches his desk) and the chance node (#182), behind which stand #178, the remainder of #229, and every varied step in town. Surface both plainly this week

Decree 10's ledger stands at rest: every co-located pair is paid face to face, and the ten far-flung ties remain lawful half-ties, told by rumor. Stage no more scenes by force — the next face-to-face comes when a move is itself a story, as Tick's was, or when the chance node lets a day lawfully bend
